creator: gwright@antiope.com
text:
(Reopening to note the cause and workaround, in case anyone else encounters this problem.)

In the current default configuration of gitlab-ce, the config/gitlab.yml file has

    # Sidekiq exporter is webserver built in to Sidekiq to expose Prometheus met
rics
    sidekiq_exporter:
      enabled: true
      log_enabled: false
      address: localhost
      port: 8082

    sidekiq_health_checks:
      enabled: true
      address: localhost
      port: 8092

While merging updates of gitlab.yml.sample into the main gitlab.yml configuration file, I made a typing error and had

   # Sidekiq exporter is webserver built in to Sidekiq to expose Prometheus met
rics
    sidekiq_exporter:
      enabled: true
      log_enabled: false
      address: localhost
      port: 8082

    sidekiq_health_checks:
      enabled: true
      address: localhost
      port: 8082

so that both sidekiq_exporter and sidekiq_health_checks were on the same port.  This caused sidekiq to crash shortly after starting with no error message.

This is not a bug in www/gitlab-ce at all; I will report the fragility of Sidekiq upstream to port collisions.  Crashing without an error message should be considered a bug.

Thank you for your help, Matthias.
